title: Item status in 2 boards
excerpt: Hi, Is there a way to automate an item if created in 1 board should be duplicated in another board and if the status changes, it automatically changes for the same ticket in other board as we…

posts.author: mohit.gambhir (mohit)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, Is there a way to automate an item if created in 1 board should be duplicated in another board and if the status changes, it automatically changes for the same ticket in other board as we…

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mohit.gambhir - have you looked into Connect Board & Mirror columns? You can setup an automation that when an item is created on Board 1, it creates an item on Board 2 and is auto-connected (one-way or two-way). Then use mirroring to show the changes.

There are limitations to this approach with some native automations/integrations. If you do not want to use these functions, you would need to look into 3rd party apps to help you out (Vlookup Auto-Link is one of our favorites).

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mohit.gambhir,

One way to do it is through Connected Boards column and then with a Mirror column.
The issue is that a Mirror column is limited in many ways.

If you’re looking to have it as a “real status” (not a mirrored one) we’ve developed an app called Connected Boards Automations. Which allows to run robust automations on connected boards column, here’s how it can be used in this case →

Screenshot 2024-07-13 at 20.26.482132×498 25.4 KB

Also worth adding that it also supports -

  1. Automatically connecting items based on conditions and flexible logic.
  2. Automatically removing connecting.
  3. Syncing values between connected items.
  4. Cross-board automations (trigger in the connected board and action in the current board)

You can find it in the monday.com app marketplace in the platform or here - https://monday.com/marketplace/listing/10000321/connected-boards-automations-

Hope that helps!

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: I think the app Same Item Multiple Boards can really help you for this use case
